title: thalictrum gallicum subsp. stipellatum
intro: Thalictrum gallicum subsp. stipellatum, also known as meadow rue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, meadow rue is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or ointment to relieve skin irritations and wounds.
